meta.title: Medical Claims Processing
meta.catalog: Guidewire ClaimCenter — Health Claims Best-Practice Catalog

steps.activity: Claim received via portal, app, email, or post
steps.output: Claim ID assigned; date of receipt recorded; acknowledgement sent to claimant
Policy active at date of loss/treatment; claimant a covered member; waiting periods checked
Claim form, medical reports, itemised bills, referral letters and receipts reviewed

steps.activity: Policy coverage and benefit limit verification
steps.output: Applicable benefit, sub-limit, excess and co-payment calculated; exclusions checked
Medical necessity, standard of care and reasonableness of charges assessed
Automated scoring; flagged claims reviewed by SIU within 3 business days
steps.controls: Key control: SIU review ≥2% of submissions

steps.output: Approved amount: covered charges less excess and co-payment; itemised breakdown
Approve, partial approve, or reject; rationale documented in Claims System
Settlement letter or rejection notice within 1 business day; amount and timeline stated
Direct bank transfer or cheque within 5 business days of approval; remittance advice sent
Status set to Settled or Rejected; loss reserve released or adjusted; GL journal posted

swim_phases.name: Claim receipt & registration
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 1–4 · all claim types
swim_phases.note: Claims arrive via portal, app, hotline or post. Incomplete claims trigger a 7-day document request.

swim_phases.name: Assessment
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 5–8 · eligibility, clinical, fraud
swim_phases.note: Routine claims auto-assess against PAS benefit configuration; complex claims get Medical Advisor review. All claims are fraud-screened in parallel.

swim_phases.name: Decision & settlement
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 9–12 · decision, payment, close
swim_phases.note: Approved claims are paid within 5 business days. Rejections cite the specific policy clause and open a 30-day appeal to the Claims Manager.

fitgap.summary_line: Overall alignment of SOP-INS-011 against the Guidewire ClaimCenter health-claims catalog
metrics: No case reserve set at FNOL
detail_slides: Steps 1–6 vs Guidewire ClaimCenter health-claims catalog
Steps 7–12 vs Guidewire ClaimCenter health-claims catalog
Steps 13–16 vs Guidewire ClaimCenter health-claims catalog

items.name: Multi-channel claim intake
items.detail: Portal, app, hotline and post intake aligns with ClaimCenter FNOL multi-channel capture.

items.name: Claim registration & ID
items.detail: Same-day registration with a unique Claim ID and dated audit aligns with ClaimCenter claim setup.

items.name: Eligibility verification
items.detail: Policy-active and covered-member checks via PAS align with ClaimCenter policy verification.

items.name: Duplicate & coordination-of-benefits check
items.detail: ClaimCenter requires duplicate-claim detection and a coordination-of-benefits (other-insurer) check at FNOL. SOP performs neither — risk of double payment.

items.name: Case reserve at registration
items.detail: ClaimCenter sets an initial case reserve when a claim is created. SOP only adjusts reserves at closure (step 12), understating outstanding-liability reporting.

items.name: Document completeness & follow-up
items.detail: Completeness check and 7-day request present, but no automated diary/escalation when documents arrive late — relies on manual follow-up.

items.name: Benefit & sub-limit calculation
items.detail: Benefit, sub-limit, excess, co-payment and exclusion checks via PAS align with ClaimCenter coverage evaluation.

items.name: Auto-adjudication of routine claims
items.detail: Routine claims are routed to manual assessment. ClaimCenter recommends a straight-through auto-adjudication rules engine for low-value, in-network, rules-clear claims.

items.name: Clinical / medical necessity review
items.detail: Adjuster and Medical Advisor review of necessity, standard of care and reasonableness aligns with ClaimCenter clinical review.

items.name: Provider network & tariff validation
items.detail: ClaimCenter validates billed charges against contracted provider tariffs/network agreements. SOP assesses 'reasonableness' subjectively with no tariff table — a key leakage driver.

items.name: Fraud screening depth
items.detail: Automated scoring and SIU review present, but no provider-level fraud profiling or network/link analytics recommended by the catalog.

items.name: Concurrent / utilisation review
items.detail: Pre-authorisation is point-in-time only. ClaimCenter recommends concurrent (utilisation) review for ongoing inpatient stays to control length-of-stay and cost.

items.name: Settlement calculation & accumulators
items.detail: Settlement net of excess/co-pay with itemised breakdown aligns; benefit accumulators tracked in PAS.

items.name: Decision & authority matrix
items.detail: Tiered approve/partial/reject decisioning with documented rationale and authority thresholds aligns with ClaimCenter decisioning.

items.name: Claimant notification & appeals
items.detail: 1-day notification, policy-clause rejection reasons and a 30-day appeal window align with the catalog communications & appeals standard.

items.name: Overpayment & COB recovery
items.detail: No recovery process for overpayments or amounts recoverable from other insurers/third parties. ClaimCenter includes a recovery/subrogation step after settlement.

critical_missing.title: Coordination of benefits (COB)
critical_missing.detail: ClaimCenter requires an other-insurance / COB check at FNOL and recovery of amounts payable by other insurers. SOP-INS-011 has neither, creating double-payment and leakage exposure.

critical_missing.title: Case reserving at FNOL
critical_missing.detail: No initial case reserve is set when a claim is registered; reserves are only touched at closure. Outstanding-claim liability and IBNR inputs are understated between registration and settlement.

critical_missing.title: Provider tariff / network pricing
critical_missing.detail: Billed charges are assessed for 'reasonableness' with no contracted-tariff reference table — a primary source of claims leakage versus the catalog's network-pricing control.

critical_missing.title: Recovery & subrogation
critical_missing.detail: No post-settlement recovery process for overpayments, third-party liability or COB amounts.

control_bars.name: Intake controls
control_bars.note: Strong multi-channel FNOL with dated audit

control_bars.name: Auto-adjudication
control_bars.note: Routine claims manually assessed; no STP engine

control_bars.name: Clinical review
control_bars.note: Adjuster + Medical Advisor for complex claims

control_bars.name: Recovery / COB
control_bars.note: No COB or subrogation recovery process

remediations.title: Add duplicate & coordination-of-benefits check at FNOL
remediations.detail: Insert a duplicate-claim and other-insurer (COB) check at registration; query prior claims and declared other cover before assessment and route COB-eligible claims for apportionment. Effort: 3 weeks (Majesco config + PAS link).

remediations.title: Introduce case reserving at registration
remediations.detail: Set an initial case reserve when each claim is created, derived from benefit type and claimed amount; adjust through the lifecycle. Aligns outstanding-liability and IBNR reporting. Effort: 4 weeks (Majesco + SAP).

remediations.title: Validate charges against provider tariffs / network
remediations.detail: Add a contracted-tariff / network-pricing reference table; flag charges above tariff for adjuster review. Primary leakage control. Effort: 5 weeks (tariff data + rules).

remediations.title: Add concurrent / utilisation review for inpatient stays
remediations.detail: Extend pre-authorisation into concurrent review for stays beyond a threshold length, with periodic clinical updates. Effort: 3 weeks.

remediations.title: Stand up auto-adjudication for routine claims
remediations.detail: Configure a straight-through rules engine to auto-settle low-value, in-network, rules-clear claims, routing only exceptions to adjusters. Effort: 6 weeks.

opt_steps.output: Completeness check; missing-item request with automated 7/14/28-day diary and escalation

opt_steps.output: Benefit, sub-limit, excess, co-payment and exclusions calculated
Straight-through rules engine settles low-value, in-network, rules-clear claims
Necessity, standard of care and reasonableness assessed
Billed charges validated against contracted tariffs; above-tariff items flagged

opt_steps.activity: Fraud screening with provider & network analytics
opt_steps.output: Claim scoring plus provider-profiling and link analytics; SIU review within 3 days
Ongoing inpatient stays reviewed periodically against length-of-stay norms
Covered charges less excess and co-payment; itemised breakdown; accumulators updated
Approve, partial or reject; rationale documented; per authority matrix
Settlement letter or rejection notice within 1 business day
Bank transfer or cheque within 5 business days; remittance advice sent

Status set; case reserve released or adjusted; GL journal posted

opt_swim_phases.name: Receipt, registration & reserving
opt_swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 1–6 · v3.0 adds COB check & reserving
opt_swim_phases.note: v3.0 adds duplicate/COB detection and case reserving at registration, with an automated missing-document diary.

opt_swim_phases.name: Assessment & adjudication
opt_swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 7–13 · auto-adjudication, tariff & concurrent review
opt_swim_phases.note: Routine claims auto-adjudicate (STP). Complex claims add provider-tariff validation and concurrent review; enhanced fraud analytics run in parallel.

opt_swim_phases.name: Decision, settlement & recovery
opt_swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 14–18 · v3.0 adds recovery step
opt_swim_phases.note: v3.0 adds a post-settlement recovery step for overpayments, coordination-of-benefits and subrogation before closure.

optimised_doc.subtitle: Optimised per Fit-Gap Analysis against the Guidewire ClaimCenter Health Claims Catalog

blocks: OPTIMISATION SUMMARY — What changed in v3.0

This version incorporates 5 remediation actions from Fit-Gap Report FG-INS-011. Alignment to the Guidewire ClaimCenter health-claims catalog increased from 71% to 92%. Critical additions: duplicate & coordination-of-benefits (COB) checking, case reserving at registration, provider tariff/network validation, concurrent utilisation review, and a post-settlement recovery process. Routine claims now auto-adjudicate.
NEW STEP (green) — step added to close a gap vs the ClaimCenter catalog
ENHANCED (amber) — existing step updated with additional controls or automation

sections.title: 1. Purpose
blocks: This Standard Operating Procedure defines the end-to-end process for receiving, validating, assessing, and settling medical insurance claims submitted by Meridian policyholders. Version 3.0 incorporates optimisations identified through fit-gap analysis against the Guidewire ClaimCenter Health Claims Best-Practice Catalog, achieving 92% alignment (up from 71% in v2.3).

Key additions in v3.0: duplicate and coordination-of-benefits checking at first notification, case reserving at registration, provider tariff validation, concurrent utilisation review for inpatient stays, straight-through auto-adjudication of routine claims, and a post-settlement recovery process for overpayments, COB and subrogation.

sections.title: 2. Scope
blocks: This procedure applies to all medical claims submitted under individual and group health, hospitalisation, and critical illness policies, including cashless treatment authorisations, reimbursement claims, pre-authorisation requests, and claim appeals.
Scope extended: coordination-of-benefits (Section 5) now precedes assessment. Claims with other applicable cover are apportioned before settlement.
This procedure does NOT cover property or casualty claims (SOP-INS-012), life insurance claims (SOP-INS-013), or internal employee health benefit claims processed by HR.

sections.title: 3. Authority Matrix
blocks: All claim settlements must be approved in accordance with the claims authority matrix. Unchanged from v2.3.

sections.title: 4. Roles and Responsibilities
blocks: Registers claims; verifies eligibility; performs duplicate/COB check; sets initial reserve; checks completeness; routes claims; notifies claimants.
Assesses complex claims; validates provider tariffs; conducts concurrent review; calculates settlement within authority.
Approves above adjuster authority; reviews rejections; manages appeals and escalations; oversees loss ratio and recovery.
Provides clinical opinion on complex, contested or high-value claims; advises on necessity and reasonable cost.
Reviews scored/flagged claims; runs provider-profiling and network analytics; investigates and recommends rejection, recovery or referral.
Executes payment runs; maintains case reserves; pursues recoveries; posts GL.

sections.title: 5. Coordination of Benefits & Reserving (New Section — v3.0)
blocks: New section. Required by the ClaimCenter catalog. A duplicate/COB check and an initial case reserve must be completed at registration before a claim proceeds to assessment.
Prior claims for same event/period identified; potential duplicates flagged
Other applicable cover (employer, government, other insurer) identified; primary/secondary payer set
Liability apportioned; secondary-payer amount calculated per policy COB clause
Case reserve raised in Majesco/SAP from benefit type and claimed amount

sections.title: 6. Claim Processing Steps
blocks: Claim ID assigned; acknowledgement within 1 business day
Policy active; covered member; waiting periods checked
Missing items requested; automated 7/14/28-day diary and escalation
Straight-through settlement of low-value, in-network, rules-clear claims
Necessity, standard of care, reasonableness assessed
Charges validated against contracted tariffs; above-tariff items flagged
Fraud screening with provider & network analytics
Scoring plus provider profiling and link analytics; SIU review ≤3 days
Ongoing stays reviewed against length-of-stay norms
Covered charges less excess and co-payment; accumulators updated
Approve, partial, or reject; rationale recorded; per authority matrix
Settlement letter or rejection notice within 1 business day
Bank transfer or cheque within 5 business days; remittance advice
Recoverable amounts identified and pursued post-settlement
Status set; reserve released/adjusted; GL journal posted

sections.title: 7. Exception Handling
blocks: Rejection notice states the specific reason with reference to the policy wording clause; issued within 1 business day of decision.
Claimant has 30 calendar days to lodge an appeal with the Claims Manager.
Appeals reviewed within 10 business days; outcome communicated in writing.
SIU takes over the claim file; normal settlement is suspended.
Claimant notified that additional information is required, without disclosing the investigation.
SIU provides an investigation report to the Claims Manager within 20 business days; the Claims Manager determines next action including recovery or referral.
Medical facility submits a pre-authorisation request via the provider portal at least 48 hours before scheduled admission; the Adjuster responds within 4 business hours.
Approval triggers a direct-billing arrangement; no cash payment is required at point of care.
For inpatient stays beyond the approved length, concurrent review (step 12) requires periodic clinical updates before further days are authorised.
Document follow-up is now driven by an automated diary with escalation at 14 and 28 days, replacing manual tracking.
Written request issued with an itemised list of missing items; claimant given 14 calendar days.
Day 14 without response: automated follow-up notice; claim held a further 14 days.
Day 28 without response: claim closed as 'Insufficient Documentation'; reopenable within 12 months.

sections.title: 8. Systems and Tools
blocks: Registration, reserving, adjudication workflow, settlement, recovery, reporting
Pre-authorisation, concurrent review, direct billing, document submission
Claim scoring, provider profiling, network/link analytics
Contracted tariffs and network pricing for charge validation

sections.title: 9. Key Controls and Audit Points
blocks: Controls expanded in v3.0: COB/duplicate gate, FNOL reserving, tariff validation, concurrent review, and post-settlement recovery.
Duplicate & COB gate: every claim is checked for prior claims and other cover before assessment.
Case reserving: an initial reserve is raised at registration and trued-up at closure.
Provider tariff validation: billed charges are validated against contracted tariffs to control leakage.
Segregation of duties: assessor ≠ payment approver ≠ recovery owner.
Auto-adjudication audit: STP-settled claims sampled monthly by the Claims Manager.
Fraud analytics: provider-profiling and network analytics supplement claim scoring; SIU review ≥2%.
Recovery: overpayments, COB and subrogation amounts identified and pursued after settlement.
Immutable audit trail: all registration, decision, payment and recovery actions logged with user ID and timestamp.

sections.title: 11. Document Control
blocks: Added cashless pre-auth sub-steps; updated KPIs for leakage control
OPTIMISED: COB & duplicate check; FNOL reserving; provider tariff validation; concurrent review; auto-adjudication; post-settlement recovery; catalog alignment 71% → 92%
